seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between St Helens Park and St Huberts Island. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: St Helens Park vs St Huberts Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in St Helens Park and St Huberts Island.
heading: St Helens Park vs St Huberts Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between St Helens Park, New South Wales and St Huberts Island, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: St Helens Park has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to St Huberts Island (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is St Helens Park or St Huberts Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: St Helens Park has a flood risk score of 28/100, while St Huberts Island scores 51/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: St Helens Park scores 24/100 for bushfire risk. St Huberts Island scores 31/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in St Helens Park or St Huberts Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. St Helens Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
